item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great old-school article - a simple, weird item with an interesting effect, the screwy internal logic of which is explored just enough to fill the concept out and take it in a surprising direction. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait if I write the shopping list all over my car i have an indestructible car?
Tale time.
+1 because well written and interesting concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Narrator: He did not remember to take the chicken out of the freezer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see this up! I still preferred the longer version with the test logs, but short and sweet is enjoyable too. Great work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Relevant Spongebob

It’s short, it’s weird, but it somehow makes sense. It’s what I like out of a skip.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. I remember this SCP from r/SCP. Good job my guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's good, for the most part at least. The description looks solid overall and the "hope you remember" phrase makes you understand why this thing behaves the way it does.

Minor nitpick:

require approval from the O5 Council

I don't think Council would bother with giving approvals directly, even if this thing is considered very dangerous. IMO level-4 researchers or Site director could be competent enough to do so.

I have very conflicting feelings about the last addendum though. To me the breach like this feels unnatural, also I don't know how I feel about this another property of the object. So novoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, it's weird, short and to the point. I'd question the fact that a security breach like the one in the addendum was allowed to happen at all, but as a concept it works so I can let it slide. Good, solid attempt for a first article. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my first attempt at writing an SCP, thank you to everyone in the forum and subreddit for giving feedback.
